rows.notes: Title at top of page, clearly written and legible. No amounts on this line.
Second line of heading, clear Latin script; no monetary values present.
Date line is legible though small; reads clearly as the starting date for the account period.
Second date line readable but the word 'revolutum' (or similar Latin) is slightly less clear; no amounts.
Section heading below the main title; script is legible though spelling is in older Latin form.
Clear section label introducing a group of fixed money rents; no monetary entry on this line itself.
Amount aligned under pounds column as '90' with no shillings or pence marks. Clear vertical alignment and digits.
Pounds '2' and shillings '15' are clearly written; no pence indicated. Parenthetical names legible.
Pounds '10' clearly in pounds column; no shillings/pence. Description and term 'five years' readable.
Pounds '5' and shillings '14' are clear. 'St. Martin's' script legible; no pence noted.

rows.description: Padbury, St. Mary Magd. three years
rows.notes: Pounds '4' and shillings '5' clearly visible; abbreviation 'Magd.' for Magdalen slightly condensed but readable.
Pounds '6' appears distinctly in pounds column; description 'a year' readable. No shillings or pence.
Pounds '6' clear. The tenant name looks like 'Coppin' (handwriting could be 'Coprin/Coppin'); no shillings/pence.
Columns align to show £6 10s 10d. The place/name 'Leverettis Close' is legible; small abbreviation 'S.t G.P.T.' is preserved as written.
New section header clearly written; introduces the variable (mixed) rents block.
Large pound amount '255' is clear. Shillings '19' and pence '9' align and are legible in the columns.

Pounds '65' and shillings '19' clear. Pence shows '1' with a small trailing half-penny mark (written as '1 1/2'); interpreted as 1 + 0.5. Handwritten halfpenny mark is smaller than main digits, slight ambiguity but context supports 1½d.
Amounts read as £19 7s 2d; digits are well aligned in their respective columns and clearly formed.

Pounds '28' and shillings '6' are clear. Pence looks like '10' with a small trailing mark that resembles a halfpenny symbol; the small mark could be noise or a half mark. Chosen 10d + 1/2d with moderate confidence because the mark is ambiguous.

Pounds '35' and shillings '1' clear. Pence appears as '11' with a small superscript fraction that reads like '3/4' (three farthings) — interpreted as 11 + 0.75d. The fractional mark is small but consistent with other quarter/three-quarter notations on the page.

Description uses a dash indicating same heading (rendered here as '— do Prickett'). Pounds '14' and shillings '2' clear. Pence reads '5' with a halfpenny mark ('5 1/2') — the half mark is smaller and somewhat faint, so confidence is moderate.
£29 6s 1d reads cleanly; the digits align well in the pounds/shillings/pence columns and fractional marks are absent.
Pounds '12' and shillings '19' are clear. Pence reads '11' with a halfpenny mark (written as '11 1/2'); the half mark is slightly condensed but consistent with other halfpennies on the page.
Pounds '11' and shillings '2' readable. Pence looks like '4' with an attached small halfpenny mark; the fraction is faint and the tenant name is somewhat cramped, so confidence is moderate to low.

Pounds '4' and shillings '5' clear. Pence appears as '1' with a small superscript that resembles '3/4' (three farthings) — the fractional mark is small and slightly blurred; assigned 0.75 with moderate uncertainty.
Subtotal line for mixed rents; large clear numbers in the totals area read as £607 15s 4¼d (quarter-penny/farthing shown as '1/4'). Alignment with the totals ruling line is unambiguous.
New section header for receipts of the present year; wording is legible though slightly stylized.
Amount reads clearly as £30 0s 0d; descriptor 'half a year' legible. Vertical alignment confirms columns.
Mirrors earlier Kidlington entry; pounds and shillings read as 2 and 15 respectively and are clear in the line.
Dash indicates 'ditto' from above; amount clearly £4 0s 0d. Short description and amount alignment are readable.
Reads as '— Do Augmentation' with amount £151 10s 0d. The dash indicates continuation; digits are large and clear.
Final subtotal for the 'Recepta anni presentis' block reads as £188 5s. The total line is clearly underlined/presented as a sum and the digits are legible.
